The `__aexit__` method for the `ThreadSensitiveContext` class needs to clean up the context that was set in `__aenter__`. In `__aenter__`, if there was no existing context, we set the current instance as the context using `SyncToAsync.thread_sensitive_context.set(self)`. The `__aexit__` method should reset the context by calling `reset()` on the token that was saved during `__aenter__`. This ensures proper cleanup of the context when exiting the context manager.

Here's the implementation:
output:
<explanation>
The `__aexit__` method for the `ThreadSensitiveContext` class needs to clean up the context that was set in `__aenter__`. In `__aenter__`, if there was no existing context, we set the current instance as the context using `SyncToAsync.thread_sensitive_context.set(self)`. The `__aexit__` method should reset the context by calling `reset()` on the token that was saved during `__aenter__`. This ensures proper cleanup of the context when exiting the context manager.

Here's the implementation:

```
async def __aexit__ (self, exc, value, tb) :
    if self.token is not None:
        SyncToAsync.thread_sensitive_context.reset(self.token)
```
